pattern: Rounding the number up so that an ever-longer trailing block becomes zeros is the only way to slash the digit sum, so try each rounding depth and keep the cheapest that succeeds.
hints: Adding the minimum amount that zeroes out the last k digits is what cuts the digit sum down.
For each k, round n up to the next multiple of ten-to-the-k and measure the cost of that jump.
Increase k until the rounded value's digit sum drops to at most target, then the addition for that k is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: given example rounding to five hundred
single digit at the target boundary
all nines forces rounding to the next power of ten
maximum n with a tight target
digit sum just above target by one

stress.description: random large n with a feasible target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10**12)
    target = rng.randint(1, 40)
    return [n, target]
stress.note: target between 1 and 40 is always feasible since rounding to a power of ten gives digit sum 1
